rulings.Case Title: HORNBLOWER YACHTS, LLC. ET AL VS. SITZMANN MORRIS & LAVIS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, September 18, 2019, Line 1, PLAINTIFFS HORNBLOWER YACHTS, LLC, ALCATRAZ CRUISES, LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Judge Schulman discloses that while in private practice, he represented Hornblower Yachts, LLC during approximately the mid-1990s, and that until approximately 2008, he was a member of a law firm with Richard Jacobs, its vice president and general counsel. These former affiliations will not preclude Judge Schulman from being fair and impartial in deciding this matter. Plaintiffs' motion for summary adjudication is denied on the ground that plaintiffs did not meet their initial burden of production in order to shift the burden to defendants. (Code Civ. Proc., § 437c, subd. (f)(1); see Linden Partners v. Wilshire Linden Assocs. (1998) 62 Cal.App.4th 508, 518 ["The court may rule whether a defendant owes or does not owe a duty to plaintiff without regard for the dispositive effect of such ruling on other issues in the litigation, except that the ruling must completely dispose of the issue of duty."].) The existence of and scope of duty are legal questions for the court. (See Merill v. Navegar, Inc. (2001) 26 Cal.4th 465, 477.) [The full tentative ruling was emailed to the parties.] Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: FRANCISCO MARTINEZ-VILLANUEVA VS. ALLIANCE CAB COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION - Notice Of Motion And Motion Fof Quash Of Summons For The Deposition Subpoena Of Alla Boykoff, Md And/Or For A Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, September 18, 2019, Line 1, DEFENDANT SIMON ROSENTHALL'S Motion for Quash of Summons for the Deposition Subpoena Of Alla Boykoff, M.D. and/or for a Protective Order. Pro Tem Judge James Fleming, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion to quash is denied. The alternative motion for protective order is granted to the following extent: The deposition of Dr. Boykoff will be limited to the following topics: (1) education, training and experience of Dr. Boykoff; (2) health conditions of defendant Rosenthall, if any, relevant to his ability to safely drive a passenger vehicle as of the date of the accident involved in the pending action; and (3) health conditions presently suffered by defendant Rosenthall, if any, which limit or preclude his ability to give an oral deposition now or in the foreseeable future, including conditions, if any, under which an oral deposition could be taken with proper regard to the safety and comfort of defendant Rosenthall. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jameshfleming@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. In addition, the prevailing party shall transmit a copy of the proposed order in Word format to the Pro Tem at the email address set forth above. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER B DOLAN ET AL VS. VANESSA CARIAS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Have Requests For Admissions Deemed Admitted Based On Code Section 2033.280, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, Request For Sanction Against Defendant Vanessa Carias In The Amount Of $6,500.00, And Declaration Of Aimee E. Kirby
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, September 18, 2019, Line 4, PLAINTIFFS CHRISTOPHER DOLAN AND DOLAN LAW FIRM, PC'S Motion To Have Requests For Admissions Deemed Admitted Based On Code Section 2033.280; Request For Sanction Against Defendant Vanessa Carias In The Amount Of $6,500.00. Pro Tem Judge James Fleming, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is unopposed and would be granted except that the moving papers do not include the requests for admission which are the subject of the motion nor the extension letter described in the moving papers. The Court will entertain an appropriate application for relief under Code of Civil Procedure Section 473 on proper notice to the non-moving party. Otherwise, the motion will be denied without prejudice to renewal or re-notice with corrected moving papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jameshfleming@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. In addition, the prevailing party shall transmit a copy of the proposed order in Word format to the Pro Tem at the email address set forth above. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: FLEXIBLE FUNDING LTD, LIABILITY CO. VS. TIJUANA'S PRODUCE, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant And Cross-Claimant Tijuana Produce, Inc. Seeking To Change Venue; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declarations Of Hector Castrellon, Argelia Castrellon, Jose M. Sandoval, Fanny Hernandez And Rosendo Gonzalez In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, September 18, 2019, Line 9, DEFENDANT TIJUANA'S PRODUCE, INC.'s Motion Seeking To Change Venue. Defendant Tijuana's Produce, Inc.'s motion to change venue under Code Civ. Proc. § 397(c) is denied. Section 397(c) is discretionary, and permits transfer from one proper county to another proper county when "the convenience of witnesses and the ends of justice would be promoted by the change." The burden is on the party seeking a change of venue to prove that both the convenience of witnesses and the ends of justice would be promoted by the change. (Corfee v. Southern California Edison Co. (1962) 202 Cal.App.2d 473, 477.) Here, defendant fails to show that the convenience of witnesses would be promoted by a change in venue from San Francisco County to Los Angeles County. Under a § 397(c) motion, "only convenience to third-party disinterested witnesses" may be considered. (Corfee, 202 Cal.App.2d at 478.) The convenience of the parties or their agents or employees may not be considered. (See also Stute v. Burinda (1981) 123 Cal.App.Supp.3d 11, 17 ["Convenience to witnesses only is important, and not the convenience of parties or employees or employees of parties"].) Thus, defendant's primary reliance on the purported inconvenience of the San Francisco forum to its own employees is misplaced. Defendant identifies only one third-party witness: Jose Guillermo Alcantar of Two Star Personnel Services ("TSPS"). The rule against party-witnesses does not apply when an employee is being called to testify by an opposing party. (J.C. Millett Co. v. Latchford -Marble Glass Co. (1959) 167 Cal.App.2d 218, 227.) TSPS has been named as a cross-defendant. A showing that the convenience of witnesses will be promoted by a change of witness must be done "through affidavits which contain something more than generalities and conclusions." (Corfee, 202 Cal.App.2d at 477.) Rather, "the supporting declarations must show (1) the name of each witness, (2) the expected testimony of each witness, and (3) facts showing why the attendance of said witnesses at trial will be inconvenient at trial and why the ends of justice would be served by a transfer." (Stute, 123 Cal.App.3d Supp. at 17.) Here, defendant has not submitted Mr. Alcantar's declaration, nor has it made any factual showing why it would be inconvenient for him to attend trial, other than the bare fact that TSPS is located in Downey in Southern California. As to defendant's prediction that unnamed representatives of other companies that defendant believes were subject to similar staffing agreements with TSPS will testify, defendant's showing does not satisfy any of the required elements, and in any event, the admissibility of such testimony in this action is doubtful at best. Defendant's motion to change venue under the doctrine of forum non conveniens is likewise denied, as inapplicable to this matter. The doctrine applies when "in the interest of substantial justice an action should be heard in a forum outside this state." (Archibald v. Cinerama Hotels (1976) 15 Cal. 3d 853, 857.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats the tentative ruling verbatim and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
